tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Most parameters are within normal limits. Notable findings include mildly decreased total CO₂, which may suggest mild metabolic acidosis or compensation; elevated LDL cholesterol and low HDL cholesterol, indicating increased cardiovascular risk. Glucose control (HbA1c, fasting glucose) and renal function (urea, creatinine, urine microalbumin/creatinine) are normal. Recommend addressing dyslipidemia and monitoring acid-base status if clinically indicated.

display.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
